---
title: "Data Summary"
author: "Hannah Farrell"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Data Summary 

```{r lib}
#Prepping the data for data summary
#Hannah 

#Prep Libraries 
remove(list = ls())

library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

#Pull in the data
df <- read.csv("MacroData_long_format_v2.csv")
view(df)

```

```{r summary}
#summmary of dataframe
dim(df)        
nrow(df)
ncol(df)
colnames(df)
str(df)

#format better
df_info <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Number of Rows", "Number of Columns"),
  Value = c(nrow(df), ncol(df))
)

kable(df_info, caption = "Dataset Dimensions")

summary_df <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Min", "1st Quartile", "Median", "Mean", "3rd Quartile", "Max"),
  Value = as.numeric(summary(df$Value))
)

kable(summary_df, caption = "Summary Statistics for Value")

```

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
summary(df$Value)


df %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

df %>%
  filter(Region != "USA") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Region, y = Value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()

 df %>%
  group_by(Industry) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

```
Note: AI was used to utilize the kable formatting of the summary stats to be more atheistic  for the report.
